summary: Ukrainian forces reported repeated guided aviation bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv, while Russian missile and UAV attacks continued across multiple fronts, including a large-scale assault on Zaporizhzhia region with 1050 strikes. Threats of Iskander-M ballistic missiles from Voronezh and other ballistic weapons from the south were announced. Casualties were confirmed in Kryvyi Rih, Sumy and Kherson from recent Russian attacks.
key_points: Ukrainian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia city (Source: @kpszsu/66050).
Russian forces carried out 1050 attacks on 56 settlements in Zaporizhzhia region, including 18 airstrikes and 769 UAVs (Source: @UkraineNow/82126).
A threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Voronezh region was reported (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68022).
Russian guided bomb strikes were also reported on Kharkiv region (Source: @kpszsu/66072).
Ukrainian authorities confirmed a missile strike in Kryvyi Rih killing three and injuring 23 (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).
A UAV attack wounded a man in Velyky Kopan, Aleshky district, Kherson region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31106).
Multiple UAV alerts were posted for Kyiv and surrounding areas, including north and Voshhorod directions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68004, @mon1tor_ua/67989).
A ballistic weapon threat from the south was announced (Source: @kpszsu/66052).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv, marking a significant escalation in air strikes. Russian forces conducted over a thousand attacks in Zaporizhzhia region and threatened Iskander-M missile launches from Voronezh, while also reporting ballistic weapon threats from the south. Casualties were confirmed in Kryvyi Rih, Sumy and Kherson, underscoring the intensifying conflict.

points: Ukrainian tactical aviation conducted guided aviation bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia city, indicating active use of precision munitions against Russian positions (Source: @kpszsu/66050).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV heading toward Chornomorsk in the Black Sea, suggesting continued reconnaissance or strike missions over maritime areas (Source: @kpszsu/66076).
Ukrainian authorities confirmed a missile attack in Kryvyi Rih resulting in three deaths and 23 injuries, highlighting the impact on civilian populations (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).
In Sumy, a Russian airstrike injured three people and ignited a trolleybus, showing the spread of attacks beyond the front lines (Source: @UkraineNow/82127).
Ukrainian reports indicated a UAV attack in Velyky Kopan, Aleshky district, Kherson region, wounding a civilian (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31106).
Ukrainian defense units reported the acquisition and deployment of 13 FPV drones on the Zaporizhzhia direction, enhancing local air defense capabilities (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67987).
Ukrainian radar alerts posted multiple reactive Shahed UAVs approaching Kyiv from the north, prompting civil defense warnings (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68004, @mon1tor_ua/67989).

Russian forces launched 1050 attacks on 56 settlements in Zaporizhzhia region, including 18 aviation strikes, 769 UAVs (mostly FPV), 260 artillery strikes and 260 rocket attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/82126).
A threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Voronezh region was announced, indicating potential long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68022).
Russian sources warned of ballistic weapon use from the southern direction, suggesting possible deployment of theater‑level missiles (Source: @kpszsu/66052).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb strikes on Kharkiv region, demonstrating continued air offensive operations (Source: @kpszsu/66072).
Russian UAV attacks were reported in Belgorod and Kursk regions, targeting civilian infrastructure such as buses and commercial facilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31105).
Russian forces claimed to have destroyed an important Ukrainian logistics earthen causeway across the Seversky Donets near Studenok (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31105).
Russian military reported the use of FPV drones against a bus on the Krasnaya Yaruga‑Vyazovoye road in Belgorod, resulting in a civilian death (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31105).
Ukrainian guided bomb strikes hit Zaporizhzhia city, a key industrial hub on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/66050).
Russian UAV attacks targeted settlements in the Dnipropetrovsk region, including a BpLA heading toward Esman/Glukhiv (Source: @kpszsu/66065).
Russian forces reported advances of up to 400 meters in the Sumy direction, occupying households near Novoy Sech and Ivolzhanske (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31105).
Ukrainian forces noted a BpLA moving north in Dnipropetrovsk towards Magdalynivka, indicating ongoing aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/66071).
Russian artillery and drone activity continued in the Kharkiv sector, with reports of explosions and fire in the city (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31105).
Ukrainian radar reported a reactive Shahed UAV over Obolon/Troieshchyna, part of the eastern defensive perimeter (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68007).
Russian forces claimed to have destroyed a Ukrainian logistics earthen causeway near Studenok, affecting supply lines on the eastern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31105).
Multiple UAV alerts indicated Shahed drones approaching Kyiv from the north and Voshhorod, prompting air defense alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68004, @mon1tor_ua/67989).
A BpLA was reported over the Dnipro area, with civilians instructed to take shelter (Source: @kpszsu/66055).
Ukrainian radar posted a reactive Shahed over the north of Kyiv, reinforcing the threat to the capital (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68004).
A BpLA was observed in the Sumy region heading toward Esman/Glukhiv, indicating aerial activity on the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/66065).
Ukrainian forces reported a BpLA near Kharkiv region passing Lymanivka on a north‑west course (Source: @kpszsu/66067).
Russian UAV attacks were noted in the Kursk region, including a strike on an outbuilding in 1st Vygornoye (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31105).
Russian forces reported infiltration into the Chervony district on the northern outskirts of Konstantinovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31105).
Russian missile and UAV attacks continued in the Kherson region, with a civilian wounded in Velyky Kopan, Aleshky district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31106).
A Russian missile strike on Kryvyi Rih killed three and injured 23, damaging industrial infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).
Ukrainian reports listed 1050 attacks in Zaporizhzhia region, including rocket and artillery strikes affecting southern territories (Source: @UkraineNow/82126).
Russian forces threatened Iskander-M missile launches from Voronezh, a southern‑adjacent region, indicating potential strikes on southern Ukrainian targets (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68022).
Ukrainian radar posted a BpLA in Zaporizhzhia heading toward Balabyne, showing aerial pressure on the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/66066).
Russian UAV attacks were reported in the Belgorod region near the southern border, targeting civilian buses (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31105).
Ukrainian forces noted a BpLA over the Black Sea heading to Chornomorsk, a strategic port in the south (Source: @kpszsu/66076).
Ukrainian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv, showing active air strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/66050, @kpszsu/66072).
Russian forces launched 769 UAVs (mostly FPV) and 18 aviation strikes in Zaporizhzhia region within 24 hours (Source: @UkraineNow/82126).
Russian reports indicated a mass drone attack on the Dubna space communications center in Moscow region, though functionality remained intact (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31105).
Russian air defense claimed to have shot down 141 enemy drones across the Russian territory in a single day (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31105).
Ukrainian defense units acquired and deployed 13 FPV drones on the Zaporizhzhia direction, enhancing local air defense (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67987).
Multiple Shahed UAVs were reported over Kyiv and surrounding areas, prompting civil defense alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68004, @mon1tor_ua/67989).
Russian air operations included strikes on a Voronezh military plant producing electronics for ballistic and cruise missiles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31105).

key_developments: Ukrainian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia city, marking a notable escalation in precision air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/66050).
Russian forces conducted 1050 attacks on Zaporizhzhia region, including 18 airstrikes and 769 UAVs, indicating a high‑intensity offensive (Source: @UkraineNow/82126).
A threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Voronezh region was announced, suggesting potential long‑range strikes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68022).
Missile strike in Kryvyi Rih resulted in three civilian deaths and 23 injuries, highlighting civilian impact (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).
Ukrainian radar reported multiple Shahed UAVs approaching Kyiv from the north, prompting air defense alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68004).
Russian guided bomb strikes were reported on Kharkiv region, extending air operations beyond Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/66072).
Russian forces threatened ballistic weapon use from the south, indicating possible theater‑level missile deployments (Source: @kpszsu/66052).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are sustaining a high tempo of combined arms attacks across multiple fronts, employing large numbers of UAVs, guided bombs, and artillery. The announced possibility of Iskander‑M missile launches from Voronezh and other ballistic threats from the south raise the risk of long‑range strikes on critical infrastructure. Ukrainian defensive measures, including the deployment of FPV drones and civil alerts, are mitigating some threats but civilian casualties continue, indicating persistent vulnerability.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have issued shelter orders in cities like Dnipro and Sumy and heightened air defense alerts around Kyiv. Local forces are integrating newly acquired FPV drones to bolster point‑defense capabilities, particularly on the Zaporizhzhia axis. International partners are monitoring the escalation, with no direct foreign military response reported in the source material.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source radar feeds continue to track reactive Shahed UAVs targeting Kyiv and surrounding oblasts, suggesting a sustained Russian UAV campaign. The concentration of guided bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv indicates a focus on disrupting logistics and command nodes. The reported threats of Iskander‑M and other ballistic weapons require monitoring of launch sites in Voronezh and southern regions for early warning.
